macro_events.event_summary: Trump extended a ceasefire in Iran, describing the Iranian government as "seriously fractured," suggesting reduced near-term risk of Middle East military escalation.
primary_drivers: Reduced geopolitical risk premium in crude oil
Risk-on rotation as Middle East tail risk fades

beneficiaries.rationale: Delta is the most jet-fuel-sensitive US legacy carrier; a crude selloff on fading Iran risk directly lowers its largest variable cost. Fuel is ~20-25% of opex and the airline does not aggressively hedge.

United has heavy international long-haul exposure including transatlantic routes that overfly/near Middle East; ceasefire extension reduces rerouting costs and fuel burn, plus direct jet fuel cost relief.
Southwest is unhedged on fuel post-2022 and domestic-only, making it a pure play on lower crude with no offsetting geopolitical revenue hit.
Cruise operator with Mediterranean itineraries; ceasefire both lowers bunker fuel costs and removes itinerary-disruption risk in the Eastern Med.
Travel booking platform benefits from lower oil (cheaper airfare stimulates bookings) and reduced Middle East/Europe travel anxiety; second-order but clean transmission.

losers.rationale: Exxon's upstream earnings lever directly to Brent; a geopolitical risk premium unwind typically pulls crude down $3-8, compressing near-term upstream cash flow. Largest US integrated, most index-weight sensitive.
Occidental is among the highest-beta US E&Ps to crude given Permian leverage and debt load; unhedged Brent exposure means ceasefire-driven selloff hits hardest.

APA has direct Egypt/offshore Suriname exposure and is a high-beta small-cap E&P that trades as a levered proxy for crude; removal of Middle East risk premium hits twice (price + its own regional assets re-rating).
Lockheed is the prime beneficiary of Middle East munitions replenishment (PAC-3, THAAD, JASSM); a durable ceasefire slows the urgency narrative around FMS orders even if backlog is unchanged.
Raytheon's missile defense franchise (Patriot, SM-series) has been bid on Iran escalation; ceasefire extension removes the near-term headline catalyst that had supported the multiple.
Northrop trades with the defense complex on Middle East headlines despite less direct munitions exposure; sector rotation out of defense on ceasefire is the mechanism.

headlines: Trump extends ceasefire in Iran, citing 'seriously fractured' Iranian government
